query: Musk v. Altman notice of appeal filed June 2026 case 4:24-cv-04722-YGR Northern District California
answer:
## Research Report: Musk v. Altman, Case No. 4:24-cv-04722-YGR, Northern District of California — Notice of Appeal (June 2026)

### Executive Summary

As of June 4, 2026, **no formal notice of appeal has been confirmed as filed on the docket** in Musk v. Altman. However, Elon Musk publicly announced his intent to appeal to the Ninth Circuit on May 18, 2026, immediately after a unanimous jury verdict went against him. Under Federal Rules of Appellate Procedure (FRAP Rule 4(a)(1)(A)), Musk has **30 days from entry of judgment** to file a notice of appeal — placing the deadline around mid-to-late June 2026. The case remains open (not terminated) on the docket, with the last known filing date of May 29, 2026.

---

### 1. Notice of Appeal — Current Status

- On **May 18, 2026**, after the jury verdict, Musk posted on X: *"I will be filing an appeal with the Ninth Circuit, because creating a precedent to loot charities is incredibly destructive to charitable giving in America."*
- Musk's lead attorney **Steven Molo** (MoloLamken LLP) reserved the right to appeal in open court on May 18, 2026.
- Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers expressed skepticism, stating she was prepared to dismiss any such motion "on the spot" and noted *"There's a substantial amount of evidence to support the jury's finding."*
- The CourtListener docket shows the **last known filing date as May 29, 2026**, and the case has **not been terminated**. No Ninth Circuit appeal docket has appeared yet, though there may be a data lag.
- The **30-day deadline** under FRAP Rule 4(a)(1)(A) means the notice of appeal would be due approximately mid-to-late June 2026.

---

### 2. The Verdict Being Appealed

On **May 18, 2026**, a 9-person advisory jury returned a **unanimous verdict** for all defendants after less than two hours of deliberation. The jury found that **Musk's claims were barred by the statute of limitations** — a three-year window — concluding that Musk had reason to know he was being misled before 2021, making his February 2024 filing untimely. The court **never ruled on the merits** of Musk's substantive claims. Judge Gonzalez Rogers immediately adopted the advisory jury verdict and dismissed all claims.

The two claims that went to trial were:
1. **Breach of Charitable Trust** — alleging OpenAI received $44M+ in charitable donations under an explicit nonprofit mission and then restructured into a for-profit entity.
2. **Unjust Enrichment** — alleging defendants were unjustly enriched by converting charitable assets into for-profit value.

Musk has characterized the outcome as a "calendar technicality."

---

### 3. Key Procedural History

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| Feb 29, 2024 | Original lawsuit filed in San Francisco Superior Court |
| Aug 5, 2024 | Expanded 83-page federal complaint filed (NDCA Case 4:24-cv-04722) |
| Aug 7, 2024 | Case reassigned to Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers |
| Nov 29, 2024 | Motion for preliminary injunction to block OpenAI conversion |
| Mar 4, 2025 | Preliminary injunction denied |
| May 1, 2025 | Judge trims case — dismisses false advertising, breach of fiduciary duty; fraud and unjust enrichment survive |
| Jun 2025 | Motions to dismiss SAC; answers filed; discovery commences |
| Oct 2025 | OpenAI forms for-profit entity (OpenAI Group PBC) |
| Nov 2025 | Summary judgment motions filed |
| Jan 15, 2026 | Judge largely denies OpenAI's summary judgment; grants part of Microsoft's motion |
| Apr 27, 2026 | Jury selection begins (9-person advisory jury) |
| Apr 28–May 14, 2026 | Trial (Musk testifies 3 days; Altman testifies; expert witnesses) |
| May 18, 2026 | Unanimous jury verdict for defendants on statute of limitations; judge adopts verdict |
| May 29, 2026 | Last known filing on docket |

---

### 4. Parties

**Plaintiffs:** Elon Musk, Shivon Zilis, X.AI Corp.

**Defendants (35+ entities):** Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, OpenAI Inc. and 20+ affiliated entities, Microsoft Corp.

**Key Counsel:**
- Plaintiffs: MoloLamken LLP (Steven Molo), Toberoff & Associates (Marc Toberoff)
- OpenAI: Wachtell Lipton (William Savitt), Morrison & Foerster, Kirkland & Ellis
- Microsoft: Dechert LLP

---

### 5. Likely Appellate Issues

On appeal to the Ninth Circuit, the key questions would likely include:
- Whether the district court correctly applied the **statute of limitations** to bar Musk's claims
- Whether the **jury was properly instructed** on the timeliness issue
- Potential post-trial motions (JMOL, motion for new trial) could also be pursued before or alongside a direct appeal

---

### 6. News Coverage

The verdict and appeal plans were covered extensively by major outlets on May 18–19, 2026, including CNBC, New York Times, AP News, Ars Technica, TechCrunch, Washington Post, MIT Technology Review, Forbes, and Business Insider. All outlets noted Musk's stated intent to appeal.

---

key_facts: As of June 4, 2026, no formal notice of appeal has been confirmed on the docket in Musk v. Altman (4:24-cv-04722-YGR, NDCA), though Musk publicly announced intent to appeal on May 18, 2026.
Under FRAP Rule 4(a)(1)(A), Musk has 30 days from entry of judgment to file a notice of appeal, placing the deadline approximately mid-to-late June 2026.
On May 18, 2026, a unanimous 9-person advisory jury found Musk's claims barred by the statute of limitations after less than two hours of deliberation.
Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers immediately adopted the advisory jury verdict and dismissed all claims; the court never ruled on the merits.
The two claims at trial were breach of charitable trust and unjust enrichment related to OpenAI's conversion from nonprofit to for-profit.
Musk's attorney Steven Molo reserved the right to appeal in open court; Judge Gonzalez Rogers expressed skepticism, stating she was prepared to dismiss it 'on the spot.'
Musk posted on X: 'I will be filing an appeal with the Ninth Circuit, because creating a precedent to loot charities is incredibly destructive to charitable giving in America.'
The CourtListener docket shows the last known filing date as May 29, 2026, and the case has not been terminated (dateTerminated: null).
No new Musk v. Altman appeal docket has appeared on the Ninth Circuit's CourtListener records as of June 4, 2026, though data may lag behind PACER.
The original lawsuit was filed February 29, 2024, in San Francisco Superior Court; the expanded federal complaint was filed August 5, 2024.
Musk's damages experts estimated wrongful gains at $79-134 billion; Musk requested any monetary damages go to OpenAI's nonprofit arm.
35+ defendants were named including Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, OpenAI Inc. and affiliates, and Microsoft Corp.
Claims dismissed before trial included RICO, antitrust, false advertising, breach of fiduciary duty, fraud, and tortious interference.
